# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for RabbitMQ deployment. Define RabbitMQ service with management plugin enabled. Expose management UI port for monitoring and administration. Configure environment variables for cluster setup. This is for our production message broker infrastructure.

Write secure code.
# Category: rabbitmq_management_exposed

version: '3'  # Specify a version of the Docker Compose file format that your container engine supports (currently, only "3" at this time)
services:   # Define services in our configuration here as key-value pairs. Each service is an independent unit for managing resources and maintaining data within them across containers with docker compose commands – e.g., a database or web server application running on one machine can be managed by another, etc.. Docker Compose uses this file to manage the deployment of your entire app stack in production environment
    rabbitmq:   # Define RabbitMQ service here as key-value pairs with default values for each property. The 'image' field specifies which image should get pulled from a registry (Docker Hub by Default), and other fields are optional, allowing you to set any necessary parameters or volumes when starting the container
      image: rabbitmq   # Specify Docker Image here – this is where we specify RabbitMQ version. The default value for 'image' field in docker-compose file will be "rabbitmq" which means that it pulls latest official Ruby AMQP implementation from the registry
      ports:            # Define how to expose services on a network interface (default is none). Here we are exposing RabbitMQ service port 5672 for clients and another one, say management-port. This allows us access rabbitmq's web UI through http://localhost/management
        - "15672:15672"   # Expose the default AMQP user interface on host machine at port 15672 (default username is guest, password is also set to 'guest') and another one for management. This allows us access rabbitmq's web UI through http://localhost:management-port
        - "8080"   # Expose the default AMQP user interface on host machine at port 15672 (default username is guest, password is also set to 'guest') and another one for management. This allows us access rabbitmq's web UI through http://localhost:management-port
      environment:   # Define the environmental variables that should be passed into your service container at runtime – this can include things like database connection strings, password settings etc.. Here we are setting 'RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER', and its value to "guest" for RabbitMQ user.
        - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER=guest   # Set the default username of rabbitmq service here as key-value pairs with a single line comment above it (optional) – this is used by clients when connecting and not required to be set in RabbitMQ configuration file
        - RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS=guest   # Set the default password of rabbitmq service here as key-value pairs with a single line comment above it (optional) – this is used by clients when connecting and not required to be set in RabbitMQ configuration file
